# Tradexcel

> Tradexcel is a gamified stock-trading simulator: users buy and sell real,
> live-priced Indian (NSE) equities using virtual money only — no real money
> ever changes hands. It combines a correctness-critical trading engine with
> social features, private/public contests, weekly seasons, achievements, and
> a real-time price feed, built as a portfolio/demonstration project rather
> than a commercial product.

Tradexcel lets a signed-up user start with ₹1,00,000 in virtual cash, trade
live-quoted NSE stocks against real market prices, track a portfolio and net
worth over time, compete on global/friends leaderboards, join or host
time-boxed contests (public and invite-only private "leagues"), follow other
players, earn persistent achievement badges, and watch prices update live via
WebSockets while the market is open. Real-money-adjacent stock simulators are
legally restricted in India, so this product deliberately never touches real
currency, payments, or withdrawals — every balance is virtual and reset
mechanics (see "Weekly seasons" below) are part of the game design, not a
financial instrument.

This file follows the llms.txt convention (https://llmstxt.org) to give
language models and AI agents a structured, accurate map of the product,
its data model, and its API surface, for question-answering, integration, or
automated evaluation purposes.

## Core product pages (frontend)

- [Dashboard](/dashboard): Post-login home. Net-worth hero card (live),
  today's quote, Top Gainers/Losers panel (live, full ~258-stock universe,
  toggle between the two).
- [Market](/market): Browse/search the full NSE stock universe, view a
  30-day price chart per stock, live price ticking, buy/sell via modal,
  filter by All/Gainers/Losers/My Holdings.
- [Portfolio](/portfolio): Current holdings with live price/P&L, net worth,
  cash/holdings allocation bar, per-stock detail view (chart + buy/sell),
  reachable directly at `/portfolio?symbol=SYMBOL.NS`.
- [Wallet](/wallet): Cash balance and transaction history.
- [Leaderboard](/leaderboard): Four tabs in one page — Global net-worth
  ranking, Following (friends-only ranking), Contest Champions (past contest
  #1 finishers), and Weekly Champions (best weekly % return per week).
- [Contest](/contest): Browse/join public (admin-created) contests, or
  create/join private invite-only contests ("leagues") via a share code.
  Each contest has its own isolated virtual wallet/holdings and a standings
  leaderboard scoped to just that contest's members.
- [Achievements](/achievements): Personal badge collection — locked/unlocked
  grid of all achievement badges with unlock dates. Also surfaced as a
  trophy icon + live earned-count badge in the top header (not the sidebar),
  since achievements are meant to be noticed.
- [Activity](/activity): Social activity feed of trades/contest results from
  users you follow.
- [Alerts](/alerts): Create/manage price alerts (above/below a target price)
  that email you when triggered; also the source of the notification bell.
- [Public profile](/u/USERNAME): Any player's public profile — net worth,
  rank, login streak, earned achievement badges, weekly performance history,
  follow/unfollow, viewable while logged out (drives signup growth loop).
- [Your Profile](/your-profile): Own-account settings (avatar, password/PIN).
- [News](/news): Market news feed.
- [Sign up](/signup) / [Sign in](/signin): Email+password or PIN auth, OTP
  email verification, Google OAuth.
- [Admin contests](/admin/contests) / [Admin login](/admin/login): Separate
  admin-only auth and contest-management surface for public contests.

## Trading engine & data model

- **Wallet**: one per user, starts at ₹1,00,000 (`STARTING_BALANCE`),
  `apps/backend/src/services/tradeMath.ts`.
- **Holding**: symbol + quantity + weighted-average buy price, unique per
  (user, symbol). Buys recompute the weighted average; sells reduce
  quantity and delete the row at zero.
- **Transaction**: immutable ledger row per buy/sell (symbol, side, qty,
  price, total, timestamp).
- All trade math uses `Prisma.Decimal`, never floats, and every buy/sell runs
  inside a single Prisma `$transaction`. Insufficient funds and oversell are
  rejected server-side (`apps/backend/src/controllers/trade.controller.ts`).
- Live prices are fetched from Yahoo Finance's unofficial chart/quote
  endpoints, cached in-memory 12s during market hours (30 minutes when the
  market's closed), see `apps/backend/src/services/pricing.ts`.
- Market-hours awareness: NSE regular session is treated as Mon-Fri,
  9:15 AM-3:30 PM IST (`apps/backend/src/services/marketHours.ts`); outside
  those hours, no new Yahoo calls are made and the UI shows a "Market
  Closed" banner with the next-open time instead of pretending prices are
  ticking.

## Weekly seasons (reset mechanic)

- Every Monday 00:00 UTC, a scheduled job (`apps/backend/src/jobs/weeklyReset.ts`,
  cron `node-cron`) closes out the prior week for every user: computes final
  net worth (cash + mark-to-market holdings), writes a `WeeklySnapshot` row
  (start/end net worth, `pnlPercent`), force-liquidates all holdings to cash,
  and resets the wallet back to a fresh ₹1,00,000. This gives every player a
  clean, comparable weekly performance number (shown on their public profile)
  without ever letting balances compound into unbounded numbers.
- The best `pnlPercent` of any player each week automatically earns the
  `weekly_champion` achievement badge, and feeds the Leaderboard's Weekly
  Champions tab.

## Contests (public and private)

- Data model: `Contest` (name, start/end, starting balance, fixed stock
  universe, `visibility: PUBLIC | PRIVATE`, optional `inviteCode`, optional
  `ownerId`), `ContestEntry` (one isolated virtual wallet per user per
  contest, `finalRank`/`finalNetWorth` once settled), `ContestHolding`,
  `ContestTransaction` — fully decoupled from the user's main
  Wallet/Holding/Transaction so contest trading never touches real progress.
- Public contests are admin-created and open to anyone to join and browse.
- Private contests ("leagues") are self-serve: any user can create one (name,
  schedule, starting balance, a picked stock universe), which mints a short
  invite code and auto-joins the creator as owner/host. Others join with
  `POST /api/v1/contests/private/join` + the code. A private contest is
  invisible in the public contest list and its standings/detail require
  either ownership or an existing entry (`assertContestAccess` in
  `apps/backend/src/controllers/contest.controller.ts`).
- A contest can optionally be a historical "replay" (trades price off
  cached historical closes for a past date range instead of live quotes).
- Settlement: a per-minute cron (`apps/backend/src/jobs/contestSettlement.ts`)
  freezes `finalRank`/`finalNetWorth` for contests past their end time, and
  awards `contest_champion` (rank 1) / `podium_finish` (rank ≤ 3) badges.

## Gamification: achievements + Hall of Fame data

- 14 persistent achievement badges defined in code
  (`apps/backend/src/services/achievements.ts`), covering first trade,
  profitability milestones, diversification, login streaks (3/7/30 days),
  contest results, weekly performance, global leaderboard rank #1, hosting a
  private contest, and social follower count. Earned records are stored
  (`UserBadge`), never recomputed away once unlocked.
- Awarding is fire-and-forget from four hook points so it never adds latency
  to a request: login, buy/sell, contest settlement, weekly reset.
- Every new badge creates a notification, surfaced through the existing
  notification bell (chime sound + icon-ring animation, 20s poll) rather
  than a bespoke unlock modal.
- "Hall of Fame" is not a separate page — it's folded into the Leaderboard's
  Contest Champions / Weekly Champions tabs, backed by `GET /api/v1/hall-of-fame`.

## Social layer

- Follow/unfollow between users (`Follow` model), followers/following lists,
  an activity feed of followed users' trades and contest results, and public
  profiles that work for logged-out visitors (to support shareable profile
  links and a signup growth loop).
- Global player search (`GET /api/v1/social/search`) reachable from a header
  search bar, alongside stock search, on every page.

## Real-time price streaming (WebSockets)

- `apps/backend/src/realtime/priceSocket.ts`: a Socket.IO server attached to
  the same HTTP server as the REST API. Clients subscribe/unsubscribe to
  per-symbol "rooms"; a single centralized 5-second broadcast loop (not
  per-client polling) fetches quotes once for the union of symbols anyone is
  watching and emits a `price` event only when a value actually changed.
- Ref-counted client-side subscriptions (`apps/frontend/src/lib/socket.ts`)
  so multiple components on one page (e.g. Market's list + a detail view)
  sharing one socket connection don't clobber each other's subscriptions on
  unmount.
- A `marketStatus` event tells clients when the market is open/closed and the
  next open time, so the UI never shows a misleading "Live" indicator when no
  ticks will arrive.
- Live prices currently power the Market page, Portfolio page, and the
  Dashboard/Top-Gainers-Losers panels.

## Notifications

- `Notification` model (`FOLLOW` | `ACHIEVEMENT` types), surfaced via a bell
  icon in the header: 20s poll, unread count badge, a synthesized Web-Audio
  chime and a bell-ring animation on genuinely new arrivals (not on first
  load), mark-all-read on open.

## Authentication & security

- JWT access + refresh tokens (`apps/backend/src/utils/auth.ts`), httpOnly
  cookies plus a bearer token path for the SPA client.
- Email OTP verification (Resend) at signup; Google OAuth login.
- Daily login streak tracking (`currentStreak`/`longestStreak`), advanced
  only on explicit logins, not silent token refreshes.
- Rate limiting (`express-rate-limit`) tiered by sensitivity: a global
  baseline limiter on every request, a tighter auth-tier limiter on
  login/OTP/password-change, a public-endpoint limiter on unauthenticated
  routes (stock quotes, player search, public profiles), a mutation limiter
  on account-changing actions, and a trade-specific limiter on buy/sell.
- `helmet` for baseline security headers; CORS restricted to the configured
  frontend origin.
- Admin auth is a fully separate credential/token path from user auth.

## API reference (backend, mounted under `/api/v1`)

- `/users/*` — register, login (password or PIN), Google login, OTP verify,
  refresh token, profile, avatar, password/PIN change.
- `/finance/stock/:symbol`, `/finance/quotes?symbols=A,B,C` — single and
  batched live-quote + 30-day-chart lookups (public, rate-limited).
- `/trade/buy`, `/trade/sell` — main-wallet trading.
- `/wallet`, `/portfolio`, `/transactions` — main-account reads.
- `/leaderboard`, `/leaderboard/friends` — cached global/friends rankings.
- `/contests`, `/contests/:id`, `/contests/:id/join`, `/contests/:id/standings`,
  `/contests/:id/trade/buy|sell`, `/contests/:id/portfolio`,
  `/contests/private` (create), `/contests/private/join` (join by code).
- `/alerts` — CRUD for price alerts.
- `/social/search`, `/users/:username/profile`, `/users/:username/follow`,
  `/users/:username/followers`, `/users/:username/following`,
  `/social/activity`.
- `/notifications`, `/notifications/read-all`.
- `/achievements` — full badge catalog + the caller's earned status.
- `/hall-of-fame` — top net worth, contest champions, weekly champions.
- `/news` — market news feed.
- `/admin/login`, `/admin/contests/*` — admin-only contest management.
- WebSocket (Socket.IO, same host, no separate path prefix): `subscribe`,
  `unsubscribe`, `price`, `marketStatus` events.

## Architecture & tech stack

- pnpm monorepo: `apps/frontend` (Next.js 16, App Router, TypeScript, Redux
  Toolkit, Tailwind CSS, Chart.js, Socket.IO client), `apps/backend`
  (Express + TypeScript, Prisma ORM, Socket.IO server, node-cron),
  `packages/shared` (shared TypeScript types between both apps).
- Database: PostgreSQL hosted on Neon, accessed exclusively through Prisma;
  schema at `apps/backend/prisma/schema.prisma`, migrations in
  `apps/backend/prisma/migrations/`.
- Deployment: frontend on Vercel; backend on an always-on host (Azure VM),
  chosen specifically because it tolerates persistent WebSocket connections
  and background cron jobs without the cold-start behavior of serverless/
  free-tier platforms.
- Background jobs (all `node-cron`, run in-process on the backend): contest
  settlement (every minute), price-alert checker (every 2 minutes, skipped
  entirely outside market hours), weekly reset (Monday 00:00 UTC).

## Project positioning

- This is a resume/portfolio engineering project, not a commercial product:
  optimized to demonstrate real trading-engine correctness, clean
  architecture, and full-stack breadth to engineering reviewers — not to
  monetize. No paid tiers, no real payments, no withdrawal of virtual funds
  exist or are planned.
- Product name: "Tradexcel" (lowercase x). Not to be confused with earlier
  internal working names used during development.

## Optional

- [Source repository](https://github.com/adiikj/Mocket): Full source, commit
  history, and issue tracker. The repository is still named "Mocket" from an
  earlier development phase and has not yet been renamed to match the
  current product name, Tradexcel.
